vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2168: Global navigation affordance to Flywheel openQuestions from anywhere

plan.title: Global navigation affordance to Flywheel openQuestions from anywhere
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: FlywheelStatus.openQuestions[] (packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts:224) is emitted every Flywheel tick but is only visible on the /flywheel page's status tab (FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx:271-279). The operator cannot see or jump to open questions from other routes, so time-sensitive non-blocking decisions (e.g. releasing MIN merges blocked on the MYN per-project UAT gate) sit unseen until they happen to open the Flywheel page.
narratives.Proposal: Add a persistent, global count-badge indicator to the app bar's right-side status group (AppChrome.tsx:190-226), styled per pan-style-guide as an operator-attention (--warning/amber) badge: hidden at 0 open questions, showing the count when >0, updating live by seeding /api/flywheel/current then applying subscribeFlywheelStatus pushes with a 5s refetch (the same pattern FlywheelPage uses, FlywheelPage.tsx:372-395). Clicking it navigates to the Flywheel tab (setActiveTab('flywheel')) and reveals the openQuestions list — forcing the status tab and scrolling a newly-anchored section (id='flywheel-open-questions') into view. Indicator and page are decoupled by a tiny signal module (lib/flywheelReveal.ts) so neither imports the other. Presentation/navigation only — no new server endpoint, RPC method, domain event, or FlywheelStatus contract field.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No change to how openQuestions are produced or emitted (server/orchestrator untouched).
- No new dedicated openQuestions drawer or route — reuse the Flywheel status tab.
- No badge in the left Sidebar (the issue asks for the top app bar); the existing flywheel-live sidebar badge (Sidebar.tsx:82,300) is untouched.
- No new server data path, RPC method, domain event, or contract field.
- No mark-as-read / dismissal / persistence of individual questions.
- No global flywheel-status store/refactor.

autoDecisions.rationale: openQuestions are explicitly non-blocking ('weigh in'), which the pan-style-guide signal table maps to 'a human must act' = amber (--warning). Red is reserved for broken/stuck/urgent. The issue's 'operator-attention / needs-you' maps to amber.

autoDecisions.summary: 0 open questions => indicator hidden (render null), not a muted badge.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue allows 'hidden or muted'; hidden gives zero footprint when there is nothing to action and avoids a persistent dead chip in the app bar.

autoDecisions.summary: Indicator owns its own live status (seed /api/flywheel/current + subscribeFlywheelStatus + 5s refetch), mirroring FlywheelPage, rather than introducing a shared flywheel store.
autoDecisions.rationale: There is no shared flywheel-status store today; FlywheelPage subscribes locally (FlywheelPage.tsx:372-395). The /ws/rpc transport is a multiplexed singleton, so a second subscriber is cheap. The refetch makes a restart/replay unable to strand a stale count. A new global store is more machinery than one badge warrants.

autoDecisions.summary: Reveal via a decoupled signal module (lib/flywheelReveal.ts), not prop-drilling through App -> AppRoutes -> FlywheelPage.
autoDecisions.rationale: A sticky-pending + listener module handles both the cold-navigation case (consumePendingReveal on FlywheelPage mount) and the warm case (subscribe while mounted), keeps AppChrome and FlywheelPage from importing each other, touches no AppRoutes threading, and is unit-testable. URL-hash deep-linking was rejected because the dashboard uses a custom pushState router and hash parsing would be fragile for an in-app affordance.

autoDecisions.summary: Placement = app bar right-side status group, between SystemHealthPill and SystemMenu.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue's suggested placement ('next to existing system indicators') and is where every other global system indicator already lives (AppChrome.tsx:190-226).

autoDecisions.summary: Reveal target = existing 'Open Questions for the Next Tick' section in the Flywheel status tab (anchored with a new id), not a new drawer or route.
autoDecisions.rationale: That section already renders each question's full text. Reusing it is the smallest change that satisfies AC2; a dedicated drawer would duplicate rendering for no added value (a NonGoal).

Both are already imported elsewhere in the frontend, so both exist in the installed lucide-react. MessageCircleQuestion reads as 'open questions'; HelpCircle is the safe fallback if the version lacks the former.

items.title: Add flywheelReveal.ts signal module (request/consume/subscribe)

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/flywheelReveal.ts exporting three functions that decouple the app-bar indicator from FlywheelPage so neither imports the other: `requestRevealOpenQuestions(): void` (sets a sticky-pending boolean AND notifies current listeners), `consumePendingReveal(): boolean` (returns the pending flag and clears it — used by FlywheelPage on mount for the cold-navigation case), and `subscribeRevealOpenQuestions(cb: () => void): () => void` (adds cb to a listener Set, returns an unsubscribe — used by FlywheelPage while mounted for the warm/already-on-page case). Module-level `let pending = false` and `const listeners = new Set<() => void>()`. No window/CustomEvent magic strings. Pure additive new file; no consumers yet.

items.title: src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/flywheelReveal.ts exists and exports requestRevealOpenQuestions, consumePendingReveal, subscribeRevealOpenQuestions; `npm run typecheck` exits 0

Unit test src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/__tests__/flywheelReveal.test.ts proves: requestReveal sets pending so a later consumePendingReveal returns true once then false; requestReveal fires all subscribed listeners; unsubscribe stops further calls
`npm run lint` exits 0 (no unused exports flagged, no explicit any)
Add id anchor to the Open Questions section in FlywheelStatusDetails

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, extend the local `Section` helper (line ~113) to accept an optional `id`: change the signature to `function Section({ title, children, id }: { title: string; children: ReactNode; id?: string })` and add `id={id}` to its `<section>` element. Then pass `id="flywheel-open-questions"` to the Open Questions section (line ~271: `<Section title="Open Questions for the Next Tick" id="flywheel-open-questions">`). Pure additive — no other Section call sites change behavior (id is optional/undefined for them).

items.title: The Open Questions section renders a DOM element with id="flywheel-open-questions" containing each question's full text

A test (create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/__tests__/FlywheelStatusDetails.test.tsx if absent) renders FlywheelStatusDetails with a status carrying >=1 openQuestions and asserts container.querySelector('#flywheel-open-questions') exists and includes the question text
`npm run typecheck` exits 0 and `npm run lint` exits 0
Build the OpenQuestionsIndicator app-bar component

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/OpenQuestionsIndicator.tsx. Props: `{ onActivate: () => void }`. Hold `status: FlywheelStatus | null` in useState. In a useEffect (mirror FlywheelPage.tsx:372-395): seed once from GET /api/flywheel/current (define a local fetchCurrentFlywheelStatus like FlywheelPage.tsx:59-62), set a 5000ms refetch interval, and subscribe via subscribeFlywheelStatus (lib/wsTransport.ts:283); clean up interval + unsubscribe on unmount. `const count = status?.openQuestions.length ?? 0;` — if count === 0 return null (hidden). Otherwise render `<button type="button" onClick={() => { requestRevealOpenQuestions(); onActivate(); }}>` (import requestRevealOpenQuestions from lib/flywheelReveal) with classes `inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 rounded-sm h-5 border px-1.5 text-[10px] font-medium badge-bg-warning badge-border-warning text-warning-foreground tabular-nums`, a `<MessageCircleQuestion className="h-3.5 w-3.5" aria-hidden="true" />` icon (lucide-react; fall back to HelpCircle only if MessageCircleQuestion is unavailable in the installed version) then `{count}`, and title + aria-label `${count} open Flywheel question${count===1?'':'s'} — click to view`. Style per pan-style-guide: rounded-sm (NOT rounded-full), font-medium (no bold), --warning operator-attention tint.

items.title: With a snapshot of N>0 openQuestions the component renders a button showing N; with 0 openQuestions, or status null, it renders nothing (returns null)

Test src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/OpenQuestionsIndicator.test.tsx (mock ../../lib/wsTransport with a captured listener per FlywheelPage.test.tsx:8-21; mock fetch for /api/flywheel/current): renders null at 0; renders the count at >0; pushing a new snapshot through the captured listener updates the count live (FR-3); clicking calls onActivate AND requestRevealOpenQuestions
The button renders with classes rounded-sm and text-warning-foreground, and renders neither rounded-full nor font-bold/font-semibold (NFR-1: no pill, no bold)
`npm run typecheck` exits 0, `npm run lint` exits 0, and the OpenQuestionsIndicator test suite passes
FlywheelPage consumes the reveal signal (force status tab + scroll)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx, add a useEffect (after the existing status-subscription effect) that wires the reveal signal. Define a local `revealOpenQuestions()` that calls the existing `setActiveTab('status')` (the FlywheelLeftTab setter, FlywheelPage.tsx:294) and, inside requestAnimationFrame, `document.getElementById('flywheel-open-questions')?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' })`. On mount: `if (consumePendingReveal()) revealOpenQuestions();` (cold navigation from another route). While mounted: `const unsub = subscribeRevealOpenQuestions(revealOpenQuestions); return unsub;` (warm — already on the Flywheel tab when the indicator is clicked). Import consumePendingReveal + subscribeRevealOpenQuestions from ../lib/flywheelReveal. No props/threading changes (decoupled via the signal module).

items.title: Given a pending reveal, navigating to the Flywheel tab shows the 'status' left tab and scrolls #flywheel-open-questions into view
When requestRevealOpenQuestions() fires while FlywheelPage is mounted, the page shows the 'status' tab and scrolls the openQuestions section into view

Test (extend src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/__tests__/FlywheelPage.test.tsx) spies Element.prototype.scrollIntoView and asserts it is called for the pending-reveal-on-mount case; the FlywheelStatusDetails mock renders a <div id="flywheel-open-questions">
`npm run typecheck` exits 0, `npm run lint` exits 0, and the FlywheelPage test suite passes
Render OpenQuestionsIndicator in the app bar and wire navigation

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/App/AppChrome.tsx: import OpenQuestionsIndicator; add `onNavigateOpenQuestions: () => void` to AppChromeProps (interface ~line 19) and destructure it; render `<OpenQuestionsIndicator onActivate={onNavigateOpenQuestions} />` in the right-side status group between `<SystemHealthPill />` and `<SystemMenu … />` (AppChrome.tsx ~210-211). In src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx: pass `onNavigateOpenQuestions={() => setActiveTab('flywheel')}` to the `<AppChrome … />` render (App.tsx:672) — setActiveTab already exists (App.tsx:358) and uses the pushState router to navigate to /flywheel. No AppRoutes changes (reveal is handled by the signal module, not prop-drilling).

items.title: The app bar renders the OpenQuestionsIndicator on every route (it self-hides when count is 0)
Clicking the indicator navigates to /flywheel and then shows the 'status' tab with the openQuestions section scrolled into view
`npm test` (root + frontend) passes with no new failures; `npm run typecheck` exits 0 and `npm run lint` exits 0
